prompts.abstract: A cylindrical object with a slightly rounded top and bottom, smooth surface, and uniform diameter throughout.
prompts.description: A cylindrical object with a slightly rounded top and bottom, smooth surface, and uniform diameter throughout.
prompts.beginner: The design features a cylindrical base, a rectangular prism with a tilted top, and a small rectangular block. These parts are combined to create a complex, multi-layered structure.
prompts.intermediate: The design consists of three main parts that are assembled to form the final shape. The first part is a cylindrical base with a length of zero point one three six, a width of zero point one three six, and a height of zero point three seven five. The second part is a rectangular prism with a tilted top, featuring a length of zero point three seven five, a width of zero point one three six, and a height of zero point one seven. The third part is a smaller rectangular block with a length of zero point zero eight four, a width of zero point zero four one, and a height of zero point one seven. These parts are combined to create a complex structure, with the cylindrical base serving as the foundation, the rectangular prism adding a unique tilted feature, and the smaller rectangular block refining the overall shape.
prompts.expert:
In the first part, create a cylindrical base by starting with a new coordinate system. Set the Euler angles to zero, zero, and negative ninety degrees, and the translation vector to zero, zero point three seven five, and zero. Draw a two-dimensional sketch on a single face, consisting of a single loop with a circle centered at zero point zero six eight two, zero point zero six eight two, and a radius of zero point zero six eight two. Scale the sketch using a factor of zero point one three six four. Rotate the scaled sketch using the same Euler angles and translate it using the same translation vector. Extrude the sketch along the normal direction by zero point one eight seven five units and in the opposite direction by the same amount to create a new solid body. The dimensions of the cylinder are length zero point one three six three six three six three six three six three three, width zero point one three six three six three six three six three six three three, and height zero point three seven five.

In the second part, construct a rectangular prism with a tilted top. Begin by creating a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to negative ninety degrees, zero, and negative ninety degrees, and the translation vector set to zero point zero six eight two, zero, and zero. Draw a two-dimensional sketch on two faces. On the first face, draw a single loop consisting of three lines: the first line starts at zero, zero and ends at zero point three seven five, zero; the second line starts at zero point three seven five, zero and ends at zero, zero point zero three four five; the third line starts at zero, zero point zero three four five and ends at zero, zero. On the second face, draw another single loop with three lines: the first line starts at zero, zero point zero nine nine and ends at zero point three seven five, zero point one three six four; the second line starts at zero point three seven five, zero point one three six four and ends at zero, zero point one three six four; the third line starts at zero, zero point one three six four and ends at zero, zero point zero nine nine. Scale the sketch using a factor of zero point three seven five. Rotate the scaled sketch using the same Euler angles and translate it using the same translation vector. Extrude the sketch along the normal direction by zero point zero eight five two units and in the opposite direction by the same amount to remove material from the existing body. The dimensions of the rectangular prism are length zero point three seven four nine nine nine nine nine nine nine nine nine nine nine, width zero point one three six three six three six three six three six three three, and height zero point one seven zero four five four five four five four five four five four one.

In the third part, construct a rectangular block. Start by creating a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to negative ninety degrees, zero, and negative ninety degrees, and the translation vector set to zero point zero six eight two, zero point zero five three eight, and zero point zero four seven seven. Draw a two-dimensional sketch on a single face, consisting of a single loop with six lines: the first line starts at zero, zero and ends at zero point zero eight four four, zero; the second line starts at zero point zero eight four four, zero and ends at zero point zero eight four four, zero point zero two zero five; the third line starts at zero point zero eight four four, zero point zero two zero five and ends at zero point zero eight four four, zero point zero four zero nine; the fourth line starts at zero point zero eight four four, zero point zero four zero nine and ends at zero, zero point zero four zero nine; the fifth line starts at zero, zero point zero four zero nine and ends at zero, zero point zero two zero five; the sixth line starts at zero, zero point zero two zero five and ends at zero, zero. Scale the sketch using a factor of zero point zero eight four four. Rotate the scaled sketch using the same Euler angles and translate it using the same translation vector. Extrude the sketch along the normal direction by zero point zero eight five two units and in the opposite direction by the same amount to remove material from the existing body. The dimensions of the rectangular block are length zero point zero eight four three seven four nine seven three one five six seven six four four seven, width zero point zero four zero nine zero nine zero nine zero nine zero nine zero nine, and height zero point one seven zero four five four five four five four five four five four one.

prompts.nli_data:
<part_1>
### Construct a Cylindrical Base

#### Step 1: Create a New Coordinate System
- Set the Euler Angles to [0.0, 0.0, -90.0].
- Set the Translation Vector to [0.0, 0.375, 0.0].

#### Step 2: Draw a 2D Sketch
- Draw a single face.
- In this face, draw a single loop.
- The loop consists of one circle.
- The circle has a center at [0.0682, 0.0682] and a radius of 0.0682.

#### Step 3: Scale the 2D Sketch
- Scale the sketch using the sketch scale of 0.1364.

#### Step 4: Transform the Scaled 2D Sketch into 3D
- Rotate the sketch using the Euler Angles [0.0, 0.0, -90.0].
- Translate the sketch using the Translation Vector [0.0, 0.375, 0.0].

#### Step 5: Extrude the 2D Sketch
- Extrude the sketch towards the normal by 0.1875 units.
- Extrude the sketch opposite the normal by 0.1875 units.
- This operation creates a new solid body.

#### Dimensions
- Length: 0.13636363636363633
- Width: 0.13636363636363633
- Height: 0.375

</part_1>

<part_2>
### Construct a Rectangular Prism with a Tilted Top

#### Step 1: Create a New Coordinate System
- Set the Euler Angles to [-90.0, 0.0, -90.0].
- Set the Translation Vector to [0.0682, 0.0, 0.0].

#### Step 2: Draw a 2D Sketch
- Draw two faces.
- **First Face:**
  - Draw a single loop.
  - The loop consists of three lines.
  - Line 1: Start at [0.0, 0.0], end at [0.375, 0.0].
  - Line 2: Start at [0.375, 0.0], end at [0.0, 0.0345].
  - Line 3: Start at [0.0, 0.0345], end at [0.0, 0.0].
- **Second Face:**
  - Draw a single loop.
  - The loop consists of three lines.
  - Line 1: Start at [0.0, 0.099], end at [0.375, 0.1364].
  - Line 2: Start at [0.375, 0.1364], end at [0.0, 0.1364].
  - Line 3: Start at [0.0, 0.1364], end at [0.0, 0.099].

#### Step 3: Scale the 2D Sketch
- Scale the sketch using the sketch scale of 0.375.

#### Step 4: Transform the Scaled 2D Sketch into 3D
- Rotate the sketch using the Euler Angles [-90.0, 0.0, -90.0].
- Translate the sketch using the Translation Vector [0.0682, 0.0, 0.0].

#### Step 5: Extrude the 2D Sketch
- Extrude the sketch towards the normal by 0.0852 units.
- Extrude the sketch opposite the normal by 0.0852 units.
- This operation removes material from the existing body.

#### Dimensions
- Length: 0.3749999999999999
- Width: 0.13636363636363633
- Height: 0.17045454545454541

</part_2>

<part_3>
### Construct a Rectangular Block

#### Step 1: Create a New Coordinate System
- Set the Euler Angles to [-90.0, 0.0, -90.0].
- Set the Translation Vector to [0.0682, 0.0538, 0.0477].

#### Step 2: Draw a 2D Sketch
- Draw a single face.
- In this face, draw a single loop.
- The loop consists of six lines.
- Line 1: Start at [0.0, 0.0], end at [0.0844, 0.0].
- Line 2: Start at [0.0844, 0.0], end at [0.0844, 0.0205].
- Line 3: Start at [0.0844, 0.0205], end at [0.0844, 0.0409].
- Line 4: Start at [0.0844, 0.0409], end at [0.0, 0.0409].
- Line 5: Start at [0.0, 0.0409], end at [0.0, 0.0205].
- Line 6: Start at [0.0, 0.0205], end at [0.0, 0.0].

#### Step 3: Scale the 2D Sketch
- Scale the sketch using the sketch scale of 0.0844.

#### Step 4: Transform the Scaled 2D Sketch into 3D
- Rotate the sketch using the Euler Angles [-90.0, 0.0, -90.0].
- Translate the sketch using the Translation Vector [0.0682, 0.0538, 0.0477].

#### Step 5: Extrude the 2D Sketch
- Extrude the sketch towards the normal by 0.0852 units.
- Extrude the sketch opposite the normal by 0.0852 units.
- This operation removes material from the existing body.

#### Dimensions
- Length: 0.08437497315676447
- Width: 0.0409090909090909
- Height: 0.17045454545454541

</part_3>
